header_summary:
The proposed modification to BIP360 addresses concerns regarding depth-zero script trees in P2MR transactions by mandating at least one merkle authentication path, thus requiring each P2MR address to maintain two spending conditions. This change aims to enhance privacy and align the efficiency of P2MR with P2TR by discouraging less private scripting methods, with preliminary discussions indicating support among protocol proponents. For more details, see the source [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/Q8YTY1ArMzia7tRvcZ5v769WPxCMxAl_0rUy-byOBjBcTd4HXj7IB7Yt4gxLbKqk7EXFWy-PuGB6QtI28qRjE5Vob-l44UmBH6L8aXInoSk=@proton.me/T/#u#m48f1bda89d1f3eeb5234f1524176ec48f8d5d7e3).

Significant revisions to BIP127 streamline its utility in the cryptocurrency ecosystem by removing the underdeveloped *Proof File Format* and upgrading its status to Complete, focusing on practical and theoretically interesting sections like the Proof Format and PSBT Extension. The community is encouraged to share feedback on these changes, detailed in the GitHub pull request [BIPs PR 2168](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2168).

Innovative ideas to enhance blockchain transaction security include the use of zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) and testing on alternative networks like testnet or signet to minimize risks. These methods aim to preserve anonymity, provide plausible deniability, and maintain uncensorability, with ongoing debates about the merits of deploying on mainnet versus testnet [source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAFC_Vt7DLZEytF72Q0EVPeg6iED3qztMXs7aX6zBNBQ5+-ceXA@mail.gmail.com).

The P2WOTS initiative advances post-quantum signatures in Bitcoin, demonstrated through extensive testing and a user-friendly signet demo available [here](https://block_opuslux.ar.io). Community engagement and feedback are essential for evaluating the integration of this proposal into the mainstream Bitcoin protocol, with further details available on the GitHub repository [here](https://github.com/opus-lux/bitcoin-proposal).

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary:
- The proposed BIP360 modification mandates merkle paths for P2MR transactions to enhance privacy.
- It aims to discourage simple script trees and align P2MR efficiency with P2TR.
- Feedback is being collected, especially from P2TRv2 advocates, to evaluate privacy impacts and UTXO fungibility.

recent_posts.title: [BIP127] announcements: removal of Proof File Format and update status to Complete
recent_posts.summary:
- BIP127 has been updated, removing the incomplete *Proof File Format*.
- The status of BIP127 has changed from Draft to Complete, signaling no further amendments.
- Community feedback on these updates is encouraged to assess their impact.

recent_posts.title: Weak Quantum Bounty Ceremony
recent_posts.summary: - Zero-knowledge proofs could enhance blockchain security without exposing funds.  - Testnets or signets are suggested for safe scheme trials, preserving mainnet integrity.  - Key scheme properties should include anonymity, plausible deniability, and uncensorability.

recent_posts.title: P2WOTS: Live Signet & Interactive Wallet Demo
recent_posts.summary:
- The P2WOTS initiative improves Bitcoin's quantum resistance using lightweight post-quantum signatures.
- A user-friendly demo and wallet interface are available online for community testing and feedback.
- The community is encouraged to engage with the project and support its broader adoption.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Witness Version 3: ML-DSA-65 Post-Quantum Key-Path Spending
recent_posts.summary:
- The new ML-DSA key commitment proposal limits cryptographic flexibility and could compromise security.
- The choice of ML-DSA-65 over ML-DSA-44, despite similar security levels, lacks clear justification.
- ML-DSA's deployment may reduce transactions per second and does not compare favorably with alternatives like SLH-DSA.

recent_posts.title: QR-based signing flow payloads in Miniscript context
recent_posts.summary:
- Feature flags in standard proposals are met with both approval and concerns.
- Session ID integration is advocated to ensure accurate signature associations.
- Stringent verification by signers is vital for correct transaction path authentication.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary: - A new draft for Testnet5 addresses previous issues and tests BIP54.  - Feedback on the draft can be submitted via GitHub or the Bitcoin Mailing List.  - This effort aims to improve Bitcoin development functionality and user experience.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The new Bitcoin consensus rule targets specific 64-byte transaction issues in Merkle Trees.
- The rule will invalidate blocks only if transactions might be confused with node preimages.
- Implementation involves a C++ function and mining procedures to ensure block validity.

active_posts.title: BIP 3 and issues on BIPs
active_posts.summary:
- Discussion on BIPs highlights flaws in current feedback systems, recommending improvements.
- Suggested categories for feedback include 'Unresolved Issues', streamlining integration into BIPs.
- Final class would exclude irrelevant feedback, focusing on substantial community concerns.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary:
- BIP-110 version 0.4.1 limits Bitcoin transaction data field sizes to 256 bytes.
- The activation client has been stable and dictates new consensus rules for scripts.
- Implementation includes multi-platform compatibility and chains validation integration.

active_posts.title: CTV-only Vault Concept v0.1.0 release
active_posts.summary:
- The discussion evaluates OP_TEMPLATEHASH's application, effectiveness, and compatibility.
- Technical examination suggests OP_TEMPLATEHASH might match current methods' performance.
- Inquiry explores potential issues in substituting OP_TEMPLATEHASH for other solutions.

active_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
active_posts.summary:
- The paper discusses a hybrid post-quantum cryptography scheme, `BoP-2`.
- `BoP-2` combines Schnorr and PQC schemes to enhance unforgeability and non-malleability.
- This method focuses on security, reducing dependency on computational hardness.

today_in_history_posts.title: SAS: Succinct Atomic Swap
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A TLA+ specification for CoinSwap was uploaded, identifying a deadlock risk.
- ZmnSCPxj proposed a new setup with timelocks and adaptor to avoid deadlock.
- The solution supports protocol safety and reduces risks of information leaks.

today_in_history_posts.title: Disclosure of a fee blackmail attack that can make a victim loose almost all funds of a non Wumbo channel and potential fixes
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A blackmail attack exploiting HTLCs on the Lightning Network was identified in July 2019.
- Solutions include lowering the max HTLCs and implementing security patches in network software.
- Suggestions for further mitigations involve managing fees and HTLC configurations strategically.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Passive and active investments involve different levels of engagement and asset management.
- Passive investing issues include asset underutilization and misalignment in owner-agent interests.
- Proposals suggest stabilizing economies by blending deflationary and inflationary currencies.
